IndividualChief Executive OfficerCertain management employees

data.title: FiEE to Acquire 51% of Yinlian Culture for $51,000, Adds $2.9 Million Convertible Loan, VIE Pacts
data.description:
FiEE signed a suite of agreements to acquire 51% of Guangzhou Yinlian Culture for $51,000 and extend a zero-interest convertible loan of up to approximately $2.9 million, which may lift its stake to 60% upon full conversion. In parallel, FiEE implemented VIE arrangements to secure effective control and economic benefits of Maltose Culture, an AI-driven music ecosystem participant, and executed a shareholder agreement to govern Yinlian Culture post-close. These steps aim to expand FiEE’s China footprint, align governance, and integrate services and IP across its music platform.

Agreement 1: FiEE to Acquire 51% of Yinlian Culture for $51,000, Adds $2.9 Million Convertible Loan, VIE Pacts

  • Agreement type: Equity purchase and zero-interest convertible loan
  • Counterparty: Guangzhou Yinlian Culture; Guangzhou Maltose Culture Communication; Guangzhou Qingniao Culture; Shenzhen Yaojin Creative Media; Cai Yuanyao; Zhang Dingcheng; Zhang Rong
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 23 2026 / Mar 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Expand AI music ecosystem and PRC presence

Agreement 2: FiEE Signs Shareholder Agreement to Govern Yinlian Culture Post-Investment

  • Agreement type: Shareholder agreement for post-investment governance
  • Counterparty: Guangzhou Yinlian Culture; Guangzhou Maltose Culture Communication; Guangzhou Qingniao Culture; Shenzhen Yaojin Creative Media; Cai Yuanyao; Zhang Dingcheng; Zhang Rong
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 23 2026 / Mar 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Set governance and rights post-investment

Agreement 3: FiEE Secures Exclusive Option to Acquire 100% of Maltose Culture

  • Agreement type: Exclusive purchase option agreement
  • Counterparty: Maltose Culture; Yang Kai; Zhang Dingcheng
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 23 2026 / Mar 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Enable future consolidation of Maltose Culture

Agreement 4: FiEE Gains Voting Control Over Maltose Culture via Irrevocable Proxy

  • Agreement type: Irrevocable proxy agreement
  • Counterparty: Maltose Culture; Yang Kai; Zhang Dingcheng
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 23 2026 / Mar 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Operating term of Maltose Culture
  • Reason: Secure control over Maltose Culture

Agreement 5: FiEE and Maltose Culture Sign Exclusive Services and IP License Agreement

  • Agreement type: Business cooperation and IP license agreement
  • Counterparty: Maltose Culture
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 23 2026 / Mar 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Integrate services and monetize IP within ecosystem

Agreement 6: FiEE Implements Equity Pledge to Secure VIE Obligations at Maltose Culture

  • Agreement type: Equity pledge agreement
  • Counterparty: Maltose Culture; Yang Kai; Zhang Dingcheng
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 23 2026 / Mar 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until VIE obligations performed
  • Reason: Secure obligations under VIE structure

Agreement 7: FiEE Obtains Spousal Consent Supporting VIE Agreements

  • Agreement type: Spousal consent related to VIE structure
  • Counterparty: Cao Yu
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 23 2026 / Mar 23 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Ensure enforceability of VIE agreements

Original SEC Filing: FiEE, Inc. [ FIEE ] - 8-K - Mar. 27, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: FluxPoint Unveils US Uranium Conversion Plan as Prices Stay Elevated
data.description:
The company has used CERAWeek 2026 to announce its intention to establish what it considers the initial uranium conversion facility in the United States after over 70 years.

That step is taken since the price of uranium is still very high compared to one year ago, amid concerns about domestic fuel capacity.

FluxPoint Targets a Major Gap in the US Nuclear Fuel Chain

FluxPoint reported that the project will be used to maintain the current reactor fleet and support advanced nuclear technologies in the future. The company further linked the initiative to energy self-sufficiency, local reactor facilities, and national security.

Mike Chilton, FluxPoint Energy founder and chief executive, said that the availability of fuel has been a constraining factor for reactors in the US and that uranium conversion was an intolerable chokepoint in a supply chain still dominated by overseas suppliers.

John Quakes flagged the development on X and pointed to FluxPoint’s launch at CERAWeek 2026. The PR Newswire release said the company is developing a domestic uranium conversion facility to help restore US capabilities in a part of the fuel cycle that has long depended on foreign providers. FluxPoint said the planned site would convert uranium oxide into uranium hexafluoride (UF6), a required step in nuclear fuel production.

Leadership and Facility Design Shape the Launch Story

Notably, the company said Chilton brings more than 30 years of leadership in nuclear fuel. That matters since this is not a simple commodity-processing proposal. Uranium conversion sits between mined uranium and the fuel production steps needed for reactor use.

A domestic facility would help close a structural gap in US nuclear infrastructure rather than just add another upstream mining project.

FluxPoint claimed that it is based in Houston, Texas, and McLean, Virginia. The company took the launch of its CERAWeek to present the project as a larger endeavor to reestablish American nuclear fuel capacity.

This pitch aligns with a broader market-wide theme: as nuclear demand rises, utilities, developers, and policymakers are taking conversion, enrichment, and fuel availability more seriously.

Uranium Prices Stay High Even After the Recent Pullback

Meanwhile, the latest one-year uranium chart showed uranium at $84.30 per pound. That is below the spike above $100 seen earlier in 2026, but it remains well above the mid-$60s levels seen around the start of the year.

The TradingEconomics chart also showed a steady rise through much of the past year before a sharp rally in early 2026 and a later pullback.

In the context, the importance of that price structure to the FluxPoint story. Uranium no longer trades at low prices, and the market has already discounted the tightening of supply conditions and the increased nuclear interest.

data.title: Spiking US diesel prices keep trucking industry stuck in years-long slump
data.description:
Key points:
  • US diesel prices rise due to Iran's control of Strait of Hormuz
  • Independent truckers slammed by flat demand, high diesel costs
  • Big trucking firms manage fuel costs using surcharges, bulk buying

By Lisa Baertlein

The U.S.-Israeli war on Iran has sent U.S. diesel prices up 50%, delaying a long-awaited trucking industry turnaround and squeezing cash flow and profits for independent big-rig drivers.

The national average diesel fuel price hit $5.38 per gallon on Friday, according to data from the American Automobile Association, up from about $3.61 a year earlier and not far behind the highest recorded price of $5.82 in June 2022. That was nearly four months after Russia invaded Ukraine.

In California, the most populous U.S. state and home to its busiest container seaports, the diesel price hit a record $7.17 per gallon on Friday. The diesel price in Washington state also soared to an all-time high of $6.55 per gallon, according to AAA data.

Transportation is on the front lines of the historic energy disruption tracing to Iran's chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow stretch of water off its southern coast through which roughly one‑fifth of the world’s oil and liquefied natural gas normally passes. While the U.S. is well supplied with diesel, prices have spiked because oil trades on a global market.

"The guys really getting squeezed are the small carriers that can't negotiate a higher rate because demand is still flat," said Dean Croke, principal analyst at freight analytics firm DAT, nodding to the U.S. trucking industry's four-year slump.

Independent truckers often work on a per-load rate that includes fuel and have less leverage when it comes to negotiating more money when diesel prices soar.

Large contract trucking firms like FedEx , JB Hunt and CH Robinson account for about 80% of the market and use surcharges to recoup higher fuel costs. Those big players also can hedge fuel risks and use their heft to negotiate lower prices. Thus far, customers are not pushing back on diesel costs, FedEx and analysts said.

Truckers often pay fuel bills soon after purchase, but customers often have 30 days or even longer to pay for the transport, creating a cost crunch, experts said.

One silver lining is that off-contract spot rates are still about 25% higher than they were a year ago, due to thousands of drivers washing out of the industry.

"That's the cushion," said Croke. "If these rates weren't higher than last year, there'd be calamity. People would be screaming like they were in 2022, when diesel hit a record high that summer."

data.title: Talon 10-K: $0 revenue, $2.625M net income from trust interest
data.description:
Talon reported no operating revenues for the period and recorded net income of $2.625 million, driven by $3.172 million of interest income on trust funds partially offset by $0.547 million of general and administrative expenses; the company has not commenced operating activities. Earnings reflect trust-generated interest rather than revenues from business operations, and there is no operating income reported. The company remains focused on completing a business combination to begin active operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.0 — no operating revenues to date.
  • Operating Income: $0.0 — no operating activities; income is non-operating trust interest.
  • Net Income: $2.625 million — comprised of $3.172 million interest income and $0.547 million general & administrative expenses.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not provided in the filing excerpts.

Business Highlights

  • Business Model: Cayman Islands blank-check company formed to identify, negotiate and complete a business combination that will constitute the commencement of operations.
  • Capital Formation: Completed an IPO and private placement; proceeds are held in a trust to fund a future business combination and post-combination operations.
  • Operational Activity: No operating revenues; activities to date have been limited to pre-transaction organizational work, target identification, due diligence and transaction planning.
  • Trust Liquidity Use: Interest on trust funds provides limited operating withdrawals to cover expenses while the majority of trust proceeds remain reserved to finance the eventual business combination.

Original SEC Filing: Talon Capital Corp. [ TLNC ] - 10-K - Mar. 27, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Sphere 2025 10-K: Revenue $11.2M, Net Loss $21.5M
data.description:
Sphere reported 2025 revenue of $11.2 million and a net loss of $21.5 million for the year ended December 31, 2025; revenue declined sharply versus 2024 largely due to the April 2024 bitcoin halving and impacts from a fleet refresh. Gross profit was reported at $2.6 million while operating results were weighed down by operating expenses, impairments and disposals that contributed to an operating loss of approximately $21.1 million. The company did not disclose net income per share in the provided sections of the filing.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $11.2 million (decrease of $5.4 million vs 2024)
  • Gross Profit: $2.6 million
  • Operating Income: -$21.1 million (approximate)
  • Net Income: -$21.5 million
  • Net Income Per Share: Not stated in the provided sections

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Revenue fell 32% year-over-year to $11.2 million in 2025, driven primarily by the April 2024 bitcoin halving and effects from a fleet refresh.
  • Channel & Strategy Shift: The company is transitioning from third-party hosting toward vertical integration, including development of a self-owned 8MW site in Iowa to lower mining costs.
  • Fleet Refresh & Efficiency: Sphere reduced the number of active miners while replacing older units; average energy efficiency improved from 27.1 J/TH to 22.0 J/TH in 2025, with a target of approximately 19.0 J/TH in 2026.
  • Production & Holdings: Bitcoin production declined 61% to 111.6 BTC in 2025; year-end holdings were approximately 37.3 BTC (fair value about $3.3 million).
  • Corporate Development: On March 5, 2026, Sphere entered a definitive agreement to acquire Cathedra to accelerate vertical integration and scale high-density compute operations.

Original SEC Filing: Sphere 3D Corp. [ ANY ] - 10-K - Mar. 27, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Soren Acquisition 10-K: $0 Revenue, $83,209 Net Loss

No earnings per share or other operating income metrics were disclosed in the filing. The company remains a blank‑check entity focused on identifying and completing a business combination.

Business Highlights

  • Capitalization & Trust: $253 million raised in the IPO and placed in trust to fund an initial Business Combination and related operations.
  • Operational Activity: Activity to date limited to corporate formation, IPO preparation, sponsor support, and identification of potential targets; no operating revenues prior to a Business Combination.

data.title: Jaguar 10-K: $0 Revenue, $(0.26) EPS, $2.3M Net Loss for Year Ended 2025
data.description:
Jaguar reported no revenue for the years ended December 31, 2025 and 2024 and recorded a net loss of $2,314,521 for 2025, producing a basic and diluted loss per share of $0.26. The company had operating expenses of $2,330,563 driving an operating loss of approximately $2.331 million. These results reflect Jaguar's status as an exploration-stage company with no production revenue recognized.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 for the years ended December 31, 2025 and 2024.
  • Gross Profit: $0 (not applicable; no revenue to derive gross profit).
  • Operating Income: $(2.331) million; total operating expenses $2,330,563 for 2025.
  • Net Income: $(2.315) million; Net Loss and Comprehensive Loss $2,314,521 for 2025.
  • Net Income Per Share: $(0.26) basic and diluted loss per share for the year ended December 31, 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Exploration focus: Continued emphasis on uranium exploration across two Argentina projects and one Colombia project.
  • Acquisitions and land consolidation: Completed acquisitions of the Berlin project (Colombia) and Argentina projects in 2024, consolidating key land positions.
  • Shift in capital deployment: In 2025 the company reallocated spending away from field exploration toward IPO readiness and corporate setup activities.
  • Cost and staffing changes: Management reduced external legal/professional fees and travel while increasing share-based compensation and accruing management bonuses.
  • Near-term funding outlook: Completed an IPO after year-end; proceeds are expected to fund approximately 24 months of planned exploration and corporate activities.

Original SEC Filing: Jaguar Uranium Corp. [ JAGU ] - 10-K - Mar. 27, 2026

data.title: Forian 2025 10-K: Revenue $30.3M, EPS (Basic & Diluted) $(0.09)
data.description:
Forian reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $30.26 million and a basic and diluted net loss per common share of $(0.09), reflecting revenue gains from the Kyber acquisition and organic growth while retaining a small net loss. Revenue increased materially year-over-year, and the company narrowed both its operating and net losses compared with 2024 despite margin pressure. These results reflect increased information licensing and processing costs alongside continued investment in products and R&D.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $30.26 million, up $10.10 million year-over-year driven by the Kyber acquisition and organic growth.
  • Gross Profit: $16.10 million; gross margin 53%, down from 64% in 2024 due to higher information licensing and processing costs.
  • Operating Loss: $(3.76) million, an improvement from $(6.99) million in 2024.
  • Net Loss: $(2.87) million, improved from $(3.77) million in 2024.
  • Basic and Diluted Net Loss Per Common Share: $(0.09), compared with $(0.12) in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth Drivers: Reported revenue rose 50% to $30.3 million in 2025, attributable to organic growth and the integration of Kyber, acquired in October 2024.
  • Channel & Geographic Mix: U.S. sales expanded to 95% of revenue in 2025, reducing contributions from Australia and other countries.
  • Product and R&D Investment: Continued investment in information products and research and development to add features and expand analytics offerings.
  • Operational Integration: Completion and integration of the Kyber acquisition materially contributed to 2025 operating results.
  • Data Sourcing and Vendor Risks: A vendor exit and licensing changes required shifts in data sourcing and contract renegotiations, affecting data inputs and operational risk management.

Original SEC Filing: Forian Inc. [ FORA ] - 10-K - Mar. 27, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Bit Digital 2025 10-K: $113.6M Revenue, $(0.31) EPS on $(84.9M) Net Loss
data.description:
Bit Digital reported $113.56 million in total revenues for the year and a net loss of $84.93 million, translating to basic and diluted loss per share of $(0.31), driven largely by operating losses as disclosed in its Form 10-K. Revenue showed a modest increase year-over-year while operating results deteriorated, reflecting a business pivot and operational reconfiguration described in the filing. The company also reported aggregate segment gross profit of $59.68 million for the period.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $113.56 million (increase of $5.51 million vs. 2024).
  • (Loss) income from operations: $(91.83) million (decline of $119.40 million vs. 2024).
  • Net (loss) income: $(84.93) million (compared to $28.31 million income in 2024).
  • (Loss) earnings per share — Basic: $(0.31); Diluted: $(0.31).
  • Segment Gross Profit (total): $59.68 million (reported aggregate segment gross profit).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix shift: Cloud services grew strongly (approximately +50% to $68.8 million), while Bitcoin mining revenue declined materially, driving a change in revenue composition toward cloud and colocation offerings.
  • Strategic pivot to ETH staking: In 2025 the company transitioned to a pure-play ETH staking and treasury model, converting BTC holdings into ETH and expanding staking activities.
  • HPC / WhiteFiber expansion: WhiteFiber cloud and colocation services ramped, with the data center portfolio targeting roughly 76 MW by the end of 2026 to support increased cloud and colocation capacity.
  • Mining fleet reconfiguration: The company reallocated its mining footprint across hosts, ending the year with about 21.4k miners (~2.8 EH/s) and disposed approximately 7.9k miners in 2025 to improve operational efficiency.
  • Commercial contracting and pipeline: Secured a long-term NC-1 services agreement (approximately $865 million over 10 years) and received more than $70 million in deferred HPC prepayments, enhancing contracted revenue visibility and supporting future operational deployments.

Original SEC Filing: Bit Digital, Inc [ BTBT ] - 10-K - Mar. 27, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Cactus Files 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing >WHD
data.description:
Cactus Inc. (WHD) filed a Form 8K - Director, Officer or Compensation Filing - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on March 27, 2026.

On March 24, 2026, Bruce Rothstein, a director of Cactus, Inc. (the "Company"), advised the Company that he will not stand for re-election at the Company's 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders. Mr. Rothstein's decision not to stand for re-election is not due to any disagreement with the Company on any matter relating to the Company's operations, policies or practices. The Company's Board of Directors has decreased the size of the Board to 8 directors effective as of immediately before the Company's 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1699136/000162828026021756/whd-20260324.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1699136/000162828026021756/0001628280-26-021756-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: California bars officials from prediction market insider betting as federal ban takes shape
data.description:
California Governor Gavin Newsom issued an executive order on Friday that bars state officials from using "non-public information" to profit on prediction market platforms.

A national ban is already being considered following a string of accurate high-value bets made shortly before major events, particularly around economic decisions and U.S. military actions.

The California order goes into effect immediately and prohibits "gubernatorial appointees from using confidential information obtained through their roles to place bets or assist others, including family members or business partners, in profiting from prediction market activity."

"Public service should not be a get-rich-quick scheme," Newsom tweeted on Friday. "While Donald Trump continues to enrich himself in office, California will stand up against corruption."

Federal push 

Similar concerns are also being voiced in Congress, with lawmakers from both sides of the aisle introducing the PREDICT Act earlier this week.

The proposal would ban members of Congress, federal officials, and their families from trading event contracts tied to "the outcomes of political events, policy decisions, and other government actions."

Violators would be forced to turn over all profits to the U.S. Treasury and pay a 10% fine on top.

This comes as stories pile up about suspiciously timed large bets made on U.S. military strikes in Venezuela and Iran have netted suspected insiders millions of dollars.

Top market players Polymarket and Kalshi are also moving to address these issues. The platforms rolled out new restrictions and surveillance measures this week that include limits on participants with direct influence over outcomes and expanded already existing rules on insider trading and market manipulation.

Prediction market platforms have grown enormously in the past year, with no signs of slowing. According to The Block data, Kalshi and Polymarket surpassed $20 billion in combined monthly trading volume for the first time this month, marking a seventh consecutive record high.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

© 2026 The Block. All Rights Reserved. This article is provided for informational purposes only. It is not offered or intended to be used as legal, tax, investment, financial, or other advice.

data.title: Dow confirms correction as traders worry about war
data.description:
By Noel Randewich

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 1.7% on Friday as investors worried about the global economic impact of the war in Iran, leaving the blue-chip index down 10% from its record high close on February 10 and confirming it has been in a correction since then.

In a broad Wall Street selloff fueled by uncertainty about the U.S. and Israeli war with Iran, the Dow in recent days has suffered its worst decline since April 2025, when U.S. President Donald Trump's "Liberation Day" global tariff announcement sent global markets into a tailspin.

On Thursday, the tech-heavy Nasdaq confirmed it has been in a correction since its record high close on October 29. The S&P 500 is down about 9% from its record high close on January 27.

While investment funds are generally not benchmarked to the Dow, the price-weighted index of 30 companies is well known among Main Street investors and its steep decline reflects a broad deterioration of investor sentiment.

With the selloff driven by fears the Middle East conflict will cripple the global economy, investors are weighing whether the downturn is a temporary dip, similar to the recovery that followed the 2025 selloff, or the start of a sustained period of risk tied to the war.

Global financial markets have tumbled and oil prices have surged since the U.S. and Israel launched a war against Iran on February 28. The Dow has lost over 7% since the attacks began. Sky-high oil prices have sparked fears of inflation, with traders expecting the Federal Reserve to be more likely to raise interest rates by year-end than cut rates, according to CME's FedWatch tool.

A 2.4% decline in Goldman Sachs Group weighed more on the Dow on Friday than any other stock.

data.title: Cue Biopharma CEO Azam Resigns, Financial and Business Chief Warren to Serve in Interim
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Cue Biopharma President and Chief Executive Usman Azam has resigned.

Chief Financial and Business Officer Lucinda Warren will stand in as interim president and chief executive while continuing her duties as finance chief, the clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company said Friday.

Azam also departs from the board, while Warren will take his place.

Azam is entitled to a lump sum payment of $232,500 related to exit, as well as three months of his base salary and 25% of his 2026 bonus.

Warren, 57, has served as chief financial and business officer since February and was chief business officer before that starting in September 2024.

Prior to joining the company, Warren was at Johnson & Johnson, beginning in 1999. She ended her time there as vice president of business development for neuroscience and Japan regionally.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Alamar Biosciences Files for Initial Public Offering
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Alamar Biosciences filed for an initial public offering.

It did not say how many shares it would list, or at what price, according to a Friday filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

The company has applied to list its shares on Nasdaq under the symbol ALMR.

The biotechnology company, which focuses on protein detection and analysis, said it would use proceeds from the IPO to expand its commercial sales and support functions, boost manufacturing capacity, and invest further in research and development.

Alamar brought in $74.2 million in revenue and posted a net loss of $29.8 million, or $1.07 a share, in 2025.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: TSX extends weekly gain as higher commodity prices offset Mideast uncertainty
data.description:
Key points:
  • TSX ends up 0.2% at 31,960.65
  • For the week, the index adds 2.1%
  • Energy rises 2.8% as oil settles 5.5% higher
  • Materials group gains 3.4%

By Fergal Smith

Canada's main stock index edged higher on Friday as higher commodity prices boosted resource shares, but uncertainty around a resolution to the Middle East conflict helped limit gains.

The S&P/TSX Composite Index ended up 73.13 points, or 0.2%, at 31,960.65. For the week, the index added 2.1%, after three straight weekly declines.

The war in the Gulf is still expected to last weeks, not months, and Washington can meet all its objectives without using ground troops, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said.

"I'm not sure any one believes that this is going to be a short-term war. And if that's the case then inflationary pressures are going to be greater," said Michael Sprung, president at Sprung Investment Management.

"The big worry in investors minds is becoming (the prospect of) higher interest rates with the possibility of recession."

Investors are leaning toward three interest rate hikes this year from the Bank of Canada, after having expected no change in rates before the conflict began. (0#CADIRPR)

U.S. consumer sentiment fell to a three-month low in March as the war stoked inflation worries and cast a shadow over the economic outlook.

The Canadian economy has already slowed, hampered by trade uncertainty.

The prospect of a more protectionist United States has spurred efforts to reduce Canada's economic reliance on its southern neighbor. Canada's minister of international trade said that he hopes to conclude a free-trade agreement with South America's Mercosur bloc by the autumn.

Energy shares rose 2.8% as the price of oil settled 5.5% higher at $99.64 a barrel. Investors have worried that a prolonged conflict could continue to disrupt energy supplies.

The materials group , which includes metal mining shares, added 3.4% as the price of gold benefited from dip buying.

Seven of the 10 major sectors ended lower, including consumer discretionary (.GSPTTCD), which lost 1.9%.

Both industrials (.GSPTTIN) and heavily weighted financials (.SPTTFS) were down 1.1%.

data.title: ICE Invests Another $600 Million in Polymarket, Stake Reaches $1.64 Billion
data.description:
Intercontinental Exchange is stepping further into prediction markets, adding another $600 million investment into Polymarket and bringing its total exposure to roughly $1.64 billion. The move appears to complete the exchange operator's earlier plan announced in October, when it committed an initial $1 billion with the option to scale up to $2 billion over time. ICE also indicated it could purchase up to $40 million in Polymarket securities from existing holders, suggesting continued alignment with the platform's longer-term growth trajectory as capital continues to flow into this emerging segment.

The broader backdrop points to rising investor interest in prediction markets, particularly following the 2024 US presidential election. Platforms offering binary, event-driven contracts across areas like geopolitics and sports have seen increased participation, with Polymarket competitor Kalshi raising more than $1 billion at a $22 billion valuation in its latest funding round. That momentum could reflect a growing view among investors that prediction markets are evolving into a scalable, high-demand category, even as policymakers flag potential risks tied to insider trading and market manipulation.

Regulation remains a developing piece of the story. The Commodity Futures Trading Commission has recently issued its first guidance on insider trading for event contracts, while maintaining a relatively hands-off approach to the sector so far. At the same time, leaders from traditional futures exchanges have pushed for tighter oversight, particularly around how prediction markets list contracts without explicit approval. Polymarket currently operates its primary platform offshore and does not accept US-based users, but is also building a US-regulated exchange that would fall under CFTC oversight, potentially shaping how the platform navigates the next phase of industry regulation.

data.title: Alamar Biosciences, Developer of Ultra-Sensitive Proteomics Instruments, Assays and Software for Biomarker Detection, Files for Nasdaq Global Market IPO
data.description:
Alamar Biosciences filed for an initial public offering on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker “ALMR.” The commercial-stage proteomics company develops ultra-sensitive instruments, assays, and software that measure low-abundance protein biomarkers in blood and other biofluids. The company plans to use proceeds for general corporate purposes, including working capital, operating expenses, and capital expenditures.

Business Description

Alamar Biosciences is a commercial-stage proteomics company focused on ultra-sensitive, multiplexed protein detection for research and future clinical applications. Its proprietary NULISA chemistry is designed to suppress background noise and reliably quantify low-abundance biomarkers in non-invasive samples such as blood, enabling what the company describes as “Precision Proteomics.” The platform centers on the ARGO HT System (research use only), an automated instrument that executes NULISA workflows for both high-plex discovery (NGS readout) and low-plex assays (qPCR readout), complemented by fully kitted consumables and analytical software.

The company sells instruments, consumables, and services. Alamar’s initial assay menu emphasizes neurology and immunology, including a CNS Disease Panel and single-plex assays such as BD-pTau217 for Alzheimer’s research. The Technology Access Program (TAP) provides fee-for-service sample processing and custom assay development to onboard customers and support complex projects. Alamar plans to extend into clinical diagnostics by developing the ARGO HT/DX instrument and pursuing FDA marketing authorization, while expanding menu content across neurology, immunology, cardiovascular and metabolic disease, oncology, and health monitoring.

Market Overview

  • Total addressable market: Proteomics research (~$19B) plus immunoassays (> $30B) today, nearly $50B combined; projected to approach $80B over the next decade.
  • Market growth: Advanced proteomics segment estimated at ~$4.1B today, with potential to reach ~$20B over the next decade.
  • Market position: Targeting precision proteomics with ultra-high sensitivity and multiplexing to bridge discovery, translational research, and future diagnostics.
  • Key competitors: Olink (Thermo Fisher), Quanterix, and mass spectrometry providers.
  • Industry trends: Protein liquid biopsy, immune monitoring, precision medicine for chronic disease, earlier disease intervention, and AI-driven biomarker discovery.

Operational Metrics

  • Customers: More than 300 customers across 25 countries, including all top 10 biopharma companies by 2024 revenue
  • Locations: Headquarters and manufacturing in Fremont, California; additional facilities in Milan, Italy and Hangzhou, China
  • Geographic presence: Direct sales in North America, Europe, and China; distributors in Australia, parts of Eastern Europe, India, Japan, Singapore, and South Korea
  • Partnerships: Multi-million-dollar collaborations with ADDF (up to $10M), Michael J. Fox Foundation, German Center for Neurodegenerative Diseases (DZNE), and the Global Neurodegeneration Proteomics Consortium (GNPC)
  • Other key metrics: Installed base of 102 instruments as of Dec 31, 2025; average annual consumables pull-through per instrument of $529,000 in 2025; >100 publications since commercial launch; direct sales team of 25 as of year-end 2025; 2025 regional revenue mix: 62% Americas, 30% EMEA, 8% APAC

Financials Highlights

  • Revenue (current): $74.2 million (2025)
  • Revenue growth: 195% year-over-year (2025 vs. 2024)
  • Gross profit: $41.7 million (56% margin)
  • Operating income: $(31.3) million (loss from operations)
  • Net income: $(29.8) million (net loss)

Management

  • Yuling Luo, Ph.D., Founder, Chief Executive Officer and Chairman - Serial entrepreneur; previously founded Advanced Cell Diagnostics (acquired by Bio‑Techne).
  • Timothy “Tod” White, President - Former investment banker and life sciences executive; previously General Counsel at Packard BioScience.
  • Justin McAnear, Chief Financial Officer - Former CFO of 10x Genomics; prior finance leadership at Tesla, Apple, and Johnson & Johnson.
  • Shiping “Steve” Chen, Ph.D., Co‑founder and Chief Operating Officer - Co‑founded Advanced Cell Diagnostics; engineering and operations leadership across life sciences companies.

IPO Structure

  • Issuer: Alamar Biosciences, Inc.
  • Filing date: March 27, 2026
  • Proposed ticker: ALMR
  • Exchange: Nasdaq Global Market
  • Lead underwriters: J.P. Morgan; BofA Securities; TD Cowen; Leerink Partners; Stifel
  • Use of proceeds: General corporate purposes, including working capital, operating expenses and capital expenditures.

Original SEC Filing: Alamar Biosciences, Inc. - S-1 - Mar. 27, 2026

Disclaimer
